commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

commit.message: feat: migrer photos candidats vers next/image pour meilleure qualité Utilise le composant Image de Next.js au lieu de pour servir des images optimisées (WebP/AVIF, tailles adaptées, support Retina). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

perf(contracts): Aggregation komplett durch Batch-Queries ersetzt

Die $lookup-Sub-Pipelines (Analysis, Events, Envelopes) verursachten
bei MongoDB Cold-Cache jeweils Full Collection Scans pro Vertrag
(50 Vertraege x 3 Collections = 150 Scans).

Neuer Ansatz:
- Step 1: countDocuments + find() parallel (statt Aggregation)
- Step 2: 3x Batch-find() mit $in parallel (statt 3x $lookup)
- Step 3: JavaScript Map-Merge (O(1) pro Vertrag)
- Neue Indexes: contract_events(contractId,status,date),
  envelopes(contractId,createdAt)
- Timing-Logs fuer Performance-Monitoring

Vorher: 150+ Collection-Scans bei Cold-Cache = 7+ Sekunden
Jetzt: 4 Index-backed Queries parallel = <500ms erwartet

commit.message:
Fix critical DB bugs, harden agent lifecycle, add error handling

Critical:
- Fix combat_instances column id → combat_id (matched schema)
- Fix world_events_log column event_type → event_id (matched schema)
- Fix session_id null on save_session_summary (add to SessionData)

Reliability:
- Add asyncio.Lock to get_pool/get_redis to prevent race conditions
- Cancel bg tasks before closing resources in on_exit
- Wrap on_exit cleanup in individual try/except blocks
- Add try/except to _build_hot_context (DB failure no longer crashes turn)
- Log exceptions from fire-and-forget tasks instead of swallowing

Error handling:
- Add @db_tool decorator to all 8 query/mechanics tools
- DB errors now return graceful JSON instead of unhandled exceptions

Correctness:
- Filter get_active_player_quests by status = 'active'
- Add update_player_hp mock to 3 more death save tests
- Fix process.env type divergence in api.ts (String() wrapper)

commit.message:
implement evidence quality evaluators using fibrosis_priors domain knowledge

Four evaluators now provide deterministic, auditable evidence scoring:
- model_relevance.py: regex-based preclinical model detection with IPF
  translational hierarchy scores; bleomycin acute checked last to avoid
  masking chronic patterns
- contradiction_detector.py: flags contested biology (M1/M2, EMT,
  myofibroblast reversibility, fibrosis resolution) without synthesizing
- evidence_quality.py: weighted composite (model 0.40, pathway 0.30,
  study_design 0.30) with full rationale audit trail
- confidence_scorer.py: applies 0.10 per-flag penalty for contested
  biology to separate epistemic caution from evidence quality

Also adds from __future__ import annotations to fibrosis_priors.py for
Python 3.9 compatibility with X | None union syntax.

commit.message:
fix(hooks): use JSON decision output for stop hook (Claude Code 2.1.59)

The plain-text `CONTINUE: ...` prefix was not recognised by Claude Code
2.1.59. Stop hooks must output JSON to control behaviour:
  {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."}  — keeps Claude running
  exit 0 (no output)                      — lets Claude stop cleanly

Rewrote all three active phases to emit the correct JSON format via jq.
Phase 4 (all reviewed, nothing pending) continues to exit 0.

Confirmed via e2e test: the plain-text format was silently ignored and
Claude exited without performing the inline review.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

are read at build time and served as static HTML. The previous deploy failure
was a one-off issue, not caused by the number of static pages.

Fix recording: start mic during countdown so stream is warm before recording

- Start mic FIRST (getUserMedia), then countdown 3-2-1, then startRecording

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: guardrails TRAMITE — applySuggestion, linkPerson e ingest nunca escriben en INGESTA

Problema: applySuggestion usaba getFirstOperacion() que tomaba la escritura
más antigua (INGESTA/antecedente). Los participantes del apunte iban al
antecedente en vez del trámite activo. Mesa quedaba vacía.

Solución:
- applySuggestion: reemplazar getFirstOperacion → getTramiteOperacion
  que filtra explícitamente por source='TRAMITE' y auto-crea si falta
- Guardrail en getTramiteOperacion: verifica source=TRAMITE antes de retornar
- Guardrail en linkPersonToOperation: verifica que operación es de TRAMITE
  antes de insertar participante, rechaza si es INGESTA
- Ingest dedup: solo busca escrituras INGESTA para evitar match con TRAMITE
- UI Mesa: badge "Origen: Antecedente" → "Vende / Transmite"
- UI Mesa: "Sin titulares en el antecedente" → "Sin transmitentes agregados"

commit.message:
Replace hardcoded LTHR percentage zones with dynamic Intervals.icu boundaries (#5)

Switches from percentage-based thresholds to athlete-specific BPM zone boundaries fetched from Intervals.icu/Garmin. Adds classifyHR() as the single source of truth for HR→zone classification, removes scattered getZoneColor/classifyZone functions, and renames hrZones→zoneTimes to clarify it holds time-in-zone data. All consumers now thread actual zone boundaries through to components. Tests updated to use dynamic boundaries via TEST_HR_ZONES.

commit.message:
Fix calendar day-of-week display and add quality troubleshooting guide

Fix day display bug and provide troubleshooting for poor schedule quality.

Issue 1: Wrong Day-of-Week Display
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Calendar showed incorrect day letters (Thu for Friday dates).

Root Cause:
- new Date("2026-05-15") parses as UTC midnight
- getDay() returns day in LOCAL time
- Timezone offset causes day to be off by 1

Fix:
- Parse date string explicitly as local time
- Split "YYYY-MM-DD" and construct local Date
- Avoids UTC/local time conversion issues
- Correct day-of-week for all timezones

Issue 2: 76 Unused Slots Analysis
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
User reported schedule with 76 unused game-capable slots.
This causes very low soft score (381/1000).

Created: SCHEDULE_QUALITY_ANALYSIS.md

Provides:
- Root cause analysis (likely over-constrained)
- Troubleshooting decision tree
- Settings to check
- Common culprits (maxGamesPerWeek too low)
- Quick fixes to try
- Expected vs actual metrics
- How to use "Generate 4 Options" to find better schedule

Likely Diagnosis:
- Constraints too tight (max 1-2 games/week)
- Guest games consuming capacity
- Blackout dates reducing availability
- Need to relax constraints or use "Generate 4 Options"

Tested locally with npm run build - passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(crm): improve error visibility when initial sync fails on mobile

Mobile users were seeing empty CRM with no explanation when initial server
sync failed (network issues, auth errors, etc.). The getLeads() function was
silently catching errors and returning empty array.

Changes:
- Added detailed console error logging with stack trace and timestamp
- Changed error handling to THROW instead of returning empty array
- UI now shows toast notification explaining sync failure
- Helps users understand why CRM appears empty on first load

This specifically fixes the "empty leads on mobile" issue where sync failures
were invisible to users.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: upgrade trueno-gpu 0.4.26 — TF32 tensor cores for 2x GEMM throughput (Refs #235) (#236)

trueno-gpu 0.4.26 enables CUBLAS_COMPUTE_32F_FAST_TF32 for all FP32 GEMMs.
TF32 uses tensor cores with 10-bit mantissa (vs 23-bit FP32) for ~2x throughput
on Ampere+ GPUs. Standard for NN training (PyTorch default since v1.7).

Affects all cuBLAS GEMMs: forward, backward_a, backward_b, attention batched.

open the CrowdReportDialog pre-filled with that tent. This drives
crowd data quality by prompting users at the moment they have
firsthand crowd level information.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): prompt crowd report for any tent selection, not only new ones

Previously the crowd report dialog only opened when a tent different
from the last saved tent was selected. This meant users who reselected
their current tent (or tapped Save without changing tent) never saw
the prompt. Now onTentSelected fires whenever any tent is saved.

commit.message:
Initial implementation of Web Research Agent

Four-step agent: Claude plans search queries → Airtop browses DuckDuckGo
→ Airtop reads top pages → Claude synthesizes a cited, grounded answer.

Every factual claim in the output is backed by a [Source N] reference
that maps to a real URL the agent visited, making hallucinations
detectable and auditable (Castellan integration point).
